steps.action: Chhau, also spelled Chhou, is a semi classical I with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Chhau, also spelled Chhou, is a semi classical I with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It is found in three styles named after the loca
steps.text: It is found in three styles named after the location where they are performed, i.

steps.action: The dance ranges from celebrating martial arts,
steps.text: The dance ranges from celebrating martial arts, acrobatics and athletics performed in festive themes of a folk dance, to a structured dance with religious themes found in Shaivism, Shaktism and Vaishnavism.

steps.action: The stories enacted by Chhau dancers include tho
steps.text: The stories enacted by Chhau dancers include those from the Hindu epics the Ramayana and the Mahabharata, the Puranas and other Indian literature.

steps.action: The dance brings together people from diverse so
steps.text: The dance brings together people from diverse socio-economic backgrounds in a festive and religious spirit.

steps.action: The Purulia Chhau dance is celebrated during the
steps.text: The Purulia Chhau dance is celebrated during the Sun festival.

steps.action: Masks form an integral part of Chhau dance in Pu
steps.text: Masks form an integral part of Chhau dance in Purulia and Seraikella styles.

steps.action: The knowledge of dance, music and mask-making is
steps.text: The knowledge of dance, music and mask-making is transmitted orally.

steps.action: The Chhau dance found in northern Odisha does no
steps.text: The Chhau dance found in northern Odisha does not use masks during the dance, but they do when the artists first appear on the stage for introduction to the audience.

steps.action: This form of the Chhau dance, states Mohan Khoka
steps.text: This form of the Chhau dance, states Mohan Khokar, has no ritual or ceremonial meaning, it is a form of community celebration and entertainment.

steps.action: The dance is performed by male dancers, at night
steps.text: The dance is performed by male dancers, at night in an open space, called akhada or asar.

steps.action: The dance is rhythmic and set to traditional fol
steps.text: The dance is rhythmic and set to traditional folk music, played on the reed pipes mohuri and shehnai.

steps.action: The themes for these dances include local legend
steps.text: The themes for these dances include local legends, folklore and episodes from the Ramayana and Mahabharata and other abstract themes.

steps.action: The precursors of Chhau dance (especially Puruli
steps.text: The precursors of Chhau dance (especially Purulia style) were not only Paika and Natua, but Nachni dance also played an important role in giving Chhau its present identity.

steps.action: Chhau dance borrows the female gaits and movemen
steps.text: Chhau dance borrows the female gaits and movements from the Nachni dance almost exclusively (Bhattacharya, 1983, Chakravarti, 2001, Kishore, 1985).

steps.action: Bose foregrounds the debate between the relation
steps.text: Bose foregrounds the debate between the relation of lasya and tandava critically in his analysis of dance in Sanskrit texts.

steps.action: While, the Seraikela and Purulia subgenres of Ch
steps.text: While, the Seraikela and Purulia subgenres of Chhau use masks during the dance, the Mayurbhanj Chhau uses none.

steps.action: The Seraikella Chhau's technique and repertoire
steps.text: The Seraikella Chhau's technique and repertoire were developed by the erstwhile nobility of this region who were both its performers and choreographers, and in the modern era people from all backgrounds dance it.

steps.action: The Seraikella Chhau is performed with symbolic
steps.text: The Seraikella Chhau is performed with symbolic masks, and the acting establishes the role the actor is playing.

steps.action: In the Mayurbhanj Chhau is performed without mas
steps.text: In the Mayurbhanj Chhau is performed without masks and is technically similar to the Seraikella Chhau.

steps.action: The Government of Odisha established the Governm
steps.text: The Government of Odisha established the Government Chhau Dance Centre in 1960 in Seraikella and the Mayurbhanj Chhau Nritya Pratisthan at Baripada in 1962.

steps.action: The Chaitra Parva festival, significant to the C
steps.text: The Chaitra Parva festival, significant to the Chhau Dance, is also sponsored by the state government.

steps.action: The Sangeet Natak Akademi has established a Nati
steps.text: The Sangeet Natak Akademi has established a National Centre for Chhau Dance at Baripada, Odisha.

steps.action: The Chhau is a semi-classical dance form from Ea
steps.text: The Chhau is a semi-classical dance form from Eastern India which has tribal and folk origins.
